{"society_power": 8.0, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "COVID emergency powers, protest/judicial-review restrictions vs. functioning checks", "reasoning": "Society's checks (courts, press, elections, markets) kept working but faced real pressure from protest restrictions, judicial-review limits, and declining civic trust, so society power slips modestly; the state gained formal authority (Brexit repatriation, emergency powers) but saw effective administrative capacity strained by fiscal weakness, netting roughly no change and keeping the UK inside the corridor with slight despotic drift."}
